question.content: For the four integrals I_1, I_2, I_3, and I_4 over the square regions split by the two diagonals, determine which integral has the largest value. Output exactly one symbol token from {I_1,I_2,I_3,I_4} in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use region symmetries in the square split by two diagonals and analyze signs of y*cos(x) in each region.
step_1.key_point: Compare I_1, I_2, I_3, I_4 by symmetry instead of full integration.

step_3.content: D_2 and D_4 are symmetric about x-axis while y*cos(x) is odd in y, so their integrals cancel to zero.

step_5.content: D_1 and D_3 are symmetric about y-axis; cos(x) is even in x, and y is positive on D_1 but negative on D_3.

step_7.content: Combine signs and zero results: only I_1 is strictly positive among all four integrals.
step_7.key_point: Maximum is attained at region D_1.
